rationale: The US-Iran deal claim by Trump with Tehran's denial is the most impactful story for Americans — potential war end or escalation affects oil prices, military posture, and domestic politics; Tren de Aragua strike is a continuation of established pattern.

top_events.title: Trump claims US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday; Tehran disputes timing
top_events.summary: Conflicting signals on an imminent Iran nuclear deal create market and policy uncertainty for Americans. A signed deal could lower oil prices and reduce military risk; failure could escalate tensions.

top_events.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike
top_events.summary: US airstrike eliminates the Tren de Aragua gang leader, continuing the established pattern of targeted strikes against criminal organizations. Modest domestic security signal, no new strategic shift.

top_events.title: Anthropic suspends new AI tools over US government security concerns
top_events.summary: Ongoing federal security review of AI tools continues to affect the US tech sector; no new qualitative escalation from prior cycle, continuation of established policy.
